slots.caption_ig:
The ghost kitchen era promised efficiency. The data shows something different: a 73% failure rate in Philadelphia, a delivery crash in San Francisco, and a warehouse graveyard in Oakland. Diners wanted to sit down all along.

#ForkFox #GhostKitchens #RestaurantIndustry #FoodData #TrendAnalysis
slots.caption_fb: We analyzed five years of ghost kitchen data across Philadelphia, San Francisco, and Oakland. The story is striking: these delivery-only operations promised lower costs and faster scaling. What we found instead was a 65-73% failure rate, margin collapse, and a quiet return to dine-in culture. Here's why the model broke and what it reveals about how people actually want to eat.
slots.video_gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16, 8s. Editorial b-roll of the topic in 'Ghost Kitchens and the Collapse of Dine-In: What the Data Reveals About the Restaurant Industry's Reckoning'. Calm pace, no music vocals. End frame: article title + 'Link in bio.'
